activities_projects.title: Analysing Poetic Devices
activities_projects.objective: Identify and explain the use of similes and metaphors
steps: Re-read the poem and highlight comparisons (e.g., 'face like a pancake')
Explain why the poet compares the man’s face to a pancake—what does it tell us about his look?
Find one more instance of imagery and describe the 'mental picture' it creates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how figurative language enhances description
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and explanation of literary devices (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Art of Hyperbole
steps: Define 'Hyperbole' (exaggeration for effect)
Identify phrases in the poem that seem impossible or exaggerated (e.g., 'grinned from ear to ear')
Write 3 original hyperbolic sentences to describe a funny character of your own
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize and apply hyperbole in writing
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of hyperbole and quality of original examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rhyme Scheme Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the rhythmic structure of the poem
steps: Assign letters (A, B, C) to the ending sounds of each line to find the rhyme scheme
Explain how the 'bouncy' rhythm matches the character of the Funny Man
Discuss in pairs if the poem would be as funny if it didn't rhyme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students master the technical analysis of rhyme and meter
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the rhyme scheme map (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: The Language of Wit
activities_projects.objective: Explore nuanced words related to humor
steps: Find 5 words in the poem related to movement or laughter
Use a dictionary to find more 'sophisticated' synonyms (e.g., instead of 'funny', use 'hilarious' or 'facetious')
Write a short paragraph about a circus using these new words
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students expand their descriptive vocabulary and use of synonyms
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct synonym selection and contextual usage (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Perspective: The Crowd
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the effect of the character on his audience
steps: Identify lines that describe the reaction of the children or the crowd
Write a 50-word response on why 'laughter' is described as a gift in the poem
Contrast the 'Funny Man' with a 'Serious Man' in 3 sentences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze the social and emotional themes of the text
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection and quality of contrast (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Direct to Indirect Speech Practice
activities_projects.objective: Apply grammar rules to poetic dialogue
steps: If the Funny Man says, 'I am the silliest man you know!', convert this to indirect speech
Find any spoken words in the poem and rewrite them as reported speech
Explain the changes made to the tense and pronouns
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice grammatical transformations within a literary context

activities_projects.title: Stanza Transformation
steps: Select one stanza of the poem
Rewrite the meaning of that stanza in two clear, formal sentences
Compare your prose version with the original poem—which one is more entertaining?
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve comprehension and formal writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of paraphrase and depth of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Extension: The Man's Backstory
activities_projects.objective: Use inference to create a narrative background
steps: The poem tells us what the man *does*. Imagine *why* he decided to be funny
Write a 5-sentence backstory for the Funny Man
Mention where he found his 'pancake face' or his 'topsy-turvy' shoes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students use creative inference based on textual clues
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and consistency with the poem's tone (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Topsy-Turvy' Dialogue
steps: Write a short dialogue (6 lines) between the Funny Man and a very serious Policeman
Use puns or 'silly logic' in the Funny Man's lines
Ensure the dialogue reflects the 'Wit and Humour' theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative dialogue writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Capture of wit and proper dialogue formatting (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Theme Mind-Map: Benefits of Humour
activities_projects.objective: Connect the poem to the broader unit theme
steps: Create a mind-map with 'The Funny Man' at the center
Branch out to show the effects he has (e.g., happiness, forgetting worries, friendship)
Add one quote from the poem to each branch
activities_projects.evaluation: Organization, visual clarity, and thematic depth (10 marks)
